subject: LISRAYA brand name must be written in ALL CAPS in all written materials
® registered symbol usage with generic name on first/most prominent mention only, external-facing docs only
Generic name format — lowercase in parentheses
Parent company sign-off
Patient-facing language — 'someone/people with dermatomyositis', not 'patients'
Abbreviation 'DM' usage after first mention of dermatomyositis; never in headlines/headings/subheads
Campaign headline lockup (verbatim)
Product benefits framework (verbatim)
MOA primary statement (verbatim)
MOA alternate statement (verbatim)
Administration statement (verbatim)
Doctor-discussion CTA pattern (verbatim)
Patient support program name
Clinical data tone — empathetic, supportive, no negative/fear-based language
Clinical claims require footnote markers with separated, noticeably smaller footnote text
Patient quote attribution format
Priovant logo, copyright line, ® legal line, job code [mm/yy], Indication and ISI live in locked header/footer
Gold or Sunshine text on White fails contrast
Coral usage restriction
Never invent gradients or ad-hoc tints
Descriptive alt text required on all images; transcripts for video/audio
Animated GIFs must carry key message in frame 1; no autoplaying motion >~5s without static fallback
Gradient callout background PNG must not be re-hosted or recolored
Headlines/headings/subheads must use full word 'dermatomyositis', never 'DM'
